test_video_source: stream-copied first 600s of test_full.mp4 (MIT 6.824 lecture, 79min total)
outcome_summary: Long-video validation reveals one real OPT-001 design flaw (translate prompt not byte-stable per-segment because targetSec varies) and confirms OPT-002 + OPT-003 work as expected at scale. Cancelled at 50% TTS completion (13/24 segments) due to retranslation oscillation on long segments — see retry_oscillation_analysis below.

opt001_long_video_validation.verdict: REVEALED REAL DESIGN FLAW — partial pass

design_flaw_discovered.title: translate system prompt is not byte-stable across segments within one job
design_flaw_discovered.explanation: internal/llm/client.go::buildTranslateSystemPrompt embeds targetSec into the SYSTEM message. Each segment has a different duration, so the cached prefix (which providers match byte-for-byte) is broken at segment N+1. As a result, translate calls always show cached=0 even though the prompt looks 'mostly identical' to a human reader.
design_flaw_discovered.fix_design: Move targetSec / Hard character limit / speech rate from system prompt into the user message. Keep system prompt = base + episode_summary only (those ARE byte-stable per job). The user message becomes 'Constraints for this segment: duration=N, char limit=K. Source language: X. Text: ...'.
design_flaw_discovered.ticket: OPT-001-followup-1 promoted to P0; was P3 followup before this validation.

secondary_observations: kimi-k2.5 returns prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens=0 across all 61 retranslate calls; not a parsing bug — kimi may genuinely lack cache in this DashScope tier, OR per-attempt prompts (history block, attempt N counter) make the prefix unstable. Both effects reinforce each other.

judge cache hits stopped growing after the initial 1024 tokens, despite 17 total calls. Suggests DashScope cache TTL is short and async judge calls (one per finished segment, with multi-second gaps) drop out of the cache window.

opt002_long_video_validation.verdict: PASS (observe-only, all judged, 0 false-positive retry verdicts)

judge_distribution.issue_type: minor awkward phrasing in long segments

_observations: Even on a long 10-minute video with 13 fully synthesised segments, judge produced ZERO 'retry' verdicts — observe-only assertion that judge does not interfere with the main loop holds.
Lowest score (0.80, segment 4) had concrete actionable issues in Chinese: '任务非单机可完成' awkward phrasing — a real quality nit, not a hallucination.
Judge issues for segments 10 and 12 (both 0.90) flagged subtle awkwardness in long Chinese translations. Human reviewer would agree these are nits worth tracking but not retry-worthy.
opt002_long_video_validation.judge_cost_estimate: $0.001-0.002 per segment at qwen-turbo DashScope rates; 17 calls ≈ $0.02 for this run.

_observations: Review fired twice (likely a worker-restart-induced stage retry; idempotent by design — see incremental-evolution.mdc#5).
Both calls succeeded via the strict tool-call path with 0 fallbacks. Robust on long-segment-list payloads (24 segments × 6 fields each ≈ 1700 input tokens per call).
Average 18 sec/call is slow for review — kimi-k2.5 is not optimised for low-latency structured output. OPT-205 (reasoning model role split) could route review to a faster model.

retry_oscillation_analysis._root_cause: 11/24 segments stuck. All have target durations 5.8-44.1 seconds. RETRANSLATION_DRIFT_THRESHOLD=0.06 with RETRANSLATION_MIN_DRIFT_THRESHOLD=0.03 produces effective threshold ~3% on long segments (e.g. 22.48s target -> ~3.3% threshold = 0.74s tolerance), which kimi-k2.5 cannot reliably hit on long Chinese translations. Pattern: 5-9 normal retries -> escalate to thinking model (600s timeout each) -> often still misses threshold.
retry_oscillation_analysis.interaction_with_opt002_judge: JUDGE verdict was 'accept' for 13/13 synthesized despite drift up to 0.115. Judge correctly recognises that 11.5% drift on a 6s segment that compresses meaning faithfully is acceptable. The DURATION-ONLY retry loop disagrees and keeps retrying — strong evidence that OPT-201 SegmentAgent should give judge VETO POWER over duration-only retries.
retry_oscillation_analysis._followup: OPT-FOLLOWUP-3 (new): tune RETRANSLATION_MIN_DRIFT_THRESHOLD upward for segments > 20s, OR let JudgeFidelity verdict='accept' short-circuit further retries even when drift exceeds threshold (depends on OPT-002 graduating from observe-only via OPT-201).

comparison_to_60s_baseline._takeaway: Long video segments take ~2x more wall time per segment due to (a) longer source text -> more retry candidates and (b) more stringent effective drift threshold. Consistent with retry oscillation analysis.

OPT-001-followup-1.status: promoted to P0 (was P3); CONCRETE FIX KNOWN
OPT-001-followup-1.task: Move targetSec / Hard char limit / speech rate from system prompt into user message in buildTranslateSystemPrompt, so system prompt becomes byte-stable per job (only varies by targetLanguage and translationSummary). Re-validate cached_tokens>0 on translate path.

OPT-001-followup-2.task: Capture streaming usage from doChatStream final SSE chunk so retranslate_thinking metrics show non-zero tokens.

OPT-FOLLOWUP-3.task: Loosen drift threshold for segments >20s, OR let judge verdict short-circuit drift retries (decision integration needed: OPT-201).

OPT-002-followup-1.task: Build golden set of >=50 hand-confirmed (src, tgt) pairs for judge correlation analysis. This 10min job alone gives 13 candidate (src, tgt, judge_score) pairs to seed it.
